questions.prompt: You pull a SIP trace for a 'no audio after answer' ticket. The trace shows INVITE → 200 OK → ACK cleanly. SDP bodies are present in INVITE and 200 OK. What's the most likely root cause?
choices: An auth problem at the PBX
RTP can't traverse NAT or firewall on the media path
The codec offered in SDP wasn't supported
Session timers expired during call setup
questions.explanation: SIP signalling completed cleanly. The audio problem is therefore RTP-side, almost always a NAT/firewall problem on the media path. Codec mismatch would cause INVITE → 488 Not Acceptable Here, not 200 OK. Auth or session-timer problems would show different shapes.

questions.prompt: A 487 Request Terminated in response to an INVITE means the call failed; you should investigate it as a problem.
questions.explanation: 487 means the caller cancelled (sent CANCEL) before the call connected. Totally normal — happens any time a user starts dialling and hangs up before answer. Not a problem.
